title: Make idmap option capable of mounting map host id to "intermediate UID"
body:
### Feature request description

option in mounting like `idmap=uids=0-1-1` map uid 0 from host filesystem to id 1 of host uid namespace. While when `userns=auto` is used, uid on host is not uid in container (and their relationship is more or less random to me).

While I want to run container in a `userns=auto` (even for containers started from root)  uid and planning to grant it some kinds of RW access to host filesystem, making idmap's map to some intermediate uid will make it easlier to be done.

### Suggest potential solution

Maybe add another option like `idmap_container_ns` to mount, and in podman when the container has a mapped uid namespace, modify the idmap before applying it to underlying filesystem.

Or make it to be able to handle `idmap=uids=1000-@0-1` and translate `@0` to uid0 in container

### Have you considered any alternatives?

I could be doing something like manually specifying the maps of user namespace and idmap, but that can be painful. Currently I am just mapping host user to the container.

### Additional context

_No response_
